originalTitle: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
summary: Carl Merriam has designed some of my favorite nostalgia-inducing Lego sets, including the Lego Nintendo Game Boy and Piranha Plant. He's assisted on the incredible Lion Knights' Castle, Galaxy Explorer, and Pirates of Barracuda Bay. Now, he's helped the company create a Donkey Kong arcade machine set that managed to win even Mario creator Shigeru […]
content: Carl Merriam has designed some of my favorite nostalgia-inducing Lego sets, including the Lego Nintendo Game Boy and Piranha Plant. He’s assisted on the incredible Lion Knights’ Castle, Galaxy Explorer, and Pirates of Barracuda Bay. Now, he’s helped the company create a Donkey Kong arcade machine set that managed to win even Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto’s favor — or so he’s told. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/lego-donkey-kong-2.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] It doesn’t play quite like Donkey Kong, of course. Jumpman, the character that would be renamed Mario, can never reach the top under his own power. But like Lego’s $230 pinball machine, it does contain a basic fully functional game. There’s a working joystick to move Mario left and right a bit, and a button to make him leap. Throw a lever, and you’ll release one of an endless series of barrels for him to jump. They’re the same original barrels that Lego released in 1989 for its first pirate sets, Merriam confirms, saying it’s his favorite thing about the set — and the biggest challenge was figuring out how to make them roll slowly enough without getting stuck. “I rolled thousands of barrels down those tracks to figure it out,” he tells The Verge. I’ll share his full answer about how the mechanism works at the bottom of this post. [Image: Levers in back let Jumpman move, and two sliding doors presumably let you clear any jammed barrels. https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/lego-donkey-kong-3.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] I’d heard that Nintendo actually works with Lego on its sets, and I was curious if Shigeru Miyamoto himself, the creator of Donkey Kong and Mario, might have been involved. Merriam says he hasn’t met Miyamoto yet — “but I did hear that he is very happy with the execution of this set.” Nintendo did have one specific piece of feedback, Merriam says: “One small request was to make the poison barrels heavier than the regular ones, so we added Minifigure heads inside them.” Theoretically, you could reunite Mario with damsel-in-distress Pauline (who has a lot more agency these days) by simply moving him with your fingers. Merriam confirms that the new Jumpman Lego element is detachable. [Image: There’s a slot to load barrels to begin with. https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/lego-donkey-kong-4.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] So, how does a single lever release endless barrels? I’ll let the designer speak for himself: > Q: How does the barrel mechanism work, and how many barrels fit inside of it at a time?  > The barrel mechanism is essentially a pump. It uses a stack of 14 Barrels going up the side of the model. Each time the barrel lever is pulled, it adds one barrel to the bottom of the stack and one falls out the top. The system holds 20 barrels so there can be 6 barrels rolling down at a time.  > Q: Do the barrels roll all the way down to the bottom? Do they fall out of the set, and need to be reinserted into the slot, or does the mechanism bring them back to the top?   > The barrels roll all the way from the top to the bottom where they are collected in a hopper that is connected to the barrel pump. This forms an infinite barrel loop which is, by definition, “barrels of fun”.  > Q: What was the biggest hurdle in designing and testing that mechanism?  > The biggest challenge was getting the barrels to roll down slowly without stopping or getting stuck. This was achieved through a combination of rolling over steps, LEGO studs, slopes, bows and using LEGO Technic beams which are slightly thinner than LEGO Bricks. This allowed us to capture the feeling of the gameplay where the barrel rolls down slowly. I rolled thousands of barrels down those tracks to figure it out… Those barrels drove me bananas!  > The second biggest hurdle was finding a way to get the barrel from the bottom to the top again. We built all kinds of stuff; things to shoot one barrel from the bottom to the top, worm gears, force multiplication levers, and more. But it turned the right answer was simply a lot more barrels.  > Q: How do the joystick and button make Jumpman/Mario move?  > The joystick moves Jumpman from left to right using a long lever up the back of the model, connected to a dingler. The jump button uses a similar mechanism used in a piano key. When it is pressed down in front of the model, the other end of a see-saw lever presses up on a long rod with Jumpman at the top. When the button is released gravity returns Jumpman to the bottom.  The Lego Donkey Kong arcade cabinet set will release August 1st for £149.99 or €169.99. At the time I typed these words, I was still waiting on pricing for US and Canada, a link to the official page, and a video. But I expect this link may work, and the leaked video below shows off the action pretty well. [Media: https://twitter.com/Wario64/status/2078612994548801826]
chineseTitle: 乐高的大金刚街机让马里奥跳过无尽的桶——据说宫本很高兴
chineseSummary: Carl Merriam 设计了一些我最喜欢的怀旧乐高套装，包括乐高任天堂游戏机和食人鱼植物。他协助制作了令人难以置信的《狮子骑士城堡》、《银河探索者》和《梭子鱼湾海盗》。现在，他帮助该公司创建了一套大金刚街机套装，甚至赢得了马里奥创造者 Shigeru [...]
chineseContent: Carl Merriam 设计了一些我最喜欢的怀旧乐高套装，包括乐高任天堂游戏机和食人鱼植物。他协助制作了令人难以置信的《狮子骑士城堡》、《银河探索者》和《梭子鱼湾海盗》。现在，他帮助该公司创建了一套大金刚街机，甚至赢得了马里奥创造者宫本茂的青睐——至少他是这么听说的。 [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/lego-donkey-kong-2.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] 当然，它玩起来不太像大金刚。 Jumpman，这个将被更名为马里奥的角色，凭借自己的力量永远无法达到顶峰。但就像乐高 230 美元的弹球机一样，它确实包含一个基本的全功能游戏。有一个有效的操纵杆可以让马里奥左右移动一点，还有一个按钮可以让他跳跃。扔一个杠杆，你就会释放出一系列无尽的桶中的一个，让他跳跃。 Merriam 证实，它们与乐高在 1989 年为其第一批海盗套装推出的原装木桶相同，并表示这是他最喜欢的东西，而最大的挑战是弄清楚如何让它们滚动得足够慢而不被卡住。 “我在这些轨道上滚动了数千个桶来找出答案，”他告诉 The Verge。我将在这篇文章的底部分享他关于该机制如何运作的完整答案。 [图片：后面的杠杆可以让跳人移动，两扇滑动门大概可以让你清除任何卡住的桶。 https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/lego-donkey-kong-3.jpg?quality=90&strip=all]我听说任天堂实际上与乐高合作开发其套装，我很好奇《大金刚》和《马里奥》的创造者宫本茂本人是否也参与其中。梅里亚姆说他还没有见过宫本茂——“但我确实听说过
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
